title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture
statement_text: Let $n, k$ be positive integers. Suppose that $G$ is a simple digraph on $n$ vertices with $\delta^+(v) \geq \lceil n/k \rceil$ for all $v \in V(G)$; then $G$ contains a directed cycle of length at most $k$.
context_text: Conjectured by Caccetta and Häggkvist in 1978, this remains open despite much study. The paper highlights the triangle case ($k=3$) — that a simple digraph on $n$ vertices with minimum out-degree at least $n/3$ contains a directed triangle — as the hardest and most characteristic case, and uses the state-of-the-art approximation (minimum out-degree $0.3465n$ suffices, Theorem 5) as a tool.
paper_contribution: Does not prove new bounds for the conjecture directly; instead uses existing approximations for the triangle case as a tool to establish improved triangularity bounds for Aharoni's rainbow generalization, and notes that improvements to those approximations would yield improvements to Theorem 3.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled.
paper_title: Improved bounds for the triangle case of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture

title: Aharoni's Rainbow Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
statement_text: Let $n, k$ be positive integers. Suppose that $G$ is a simple graph on $n$ vertices. Let $b$ be a coloring of the edges of $G$ with $n$ color classes of size at least $k$; then $G$ has rainbow girth at most $\lceil n/k \rceil$.
context_text: Proposed by Aharoni in [2] as a generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture. The paper focuses on the triangle case, studying which pairs $(\alpha, \beta)$ are triangular, meaning every simple edge-colored graph on $n$ vertices with at least $\alpha n$ color classes each of size at least $\beta n$ contains a rainbow triangle. Prior results (Aharoni, Holzman, and DeVos [2]) established that $(9/8, 1/3)$ and $(1, 2/5)$ are triangular.
paper_contribution: Improves the known triangular pairs: shows $(1.1077, 1/3)$ and $(1, 0.3988)$ are triangular, improving the respective bounds of $(9/8, 1/3)$ and $(1, 2/5)$; additionally shows $(1.3481, 1/4)$ is triangular, extending results to the regime $\beta < 1/3$.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled.
paper_title: Improved bounds for the triangle case of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture

title: Open problem on happy triples for $l < k/2$
statement_text: It would be interesting to consider the best upper bound on the number of happy triples in a graph with $k$ edges and maximum degree at most $l$, for the regime when $l < k/2$.
context_text: Lemma 2 establishes a tight bound on happy triples when $l \geq k/2$, achieved by specific extremal graphs. The authors note that their algorithmic/dynamic-programming approach gives little insight into the $l < k/2$ regime; for example, for $l=3$ and $k=3t$ the extremal graph output is $K_{t,3}$, which does not actually have maximum degree 3 for $t \geq 4$.
notes: Stated informally in the text as 'It would be interesting to consider…'; PDF source — math may be garbled.
paper_title: Improved bounds for the triangle case of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture
